name: pwa-expert
description: PWA architectural opinions and decision frameworks

opinions.title: Caching Strategy Selection
opinions.description: How to choose the appropriate caching strategy based on resource type and requirements
opinions.rationale: Different resources have different freshness requirements. Static assets benefit from cache-first (fastest), APIs need network-first (freshest), semi-static content works with stale-while-revalidate (balanced)

options.name: Cache First (Cache Falling Back to Network)
options.description: Check cache first, use network as fallback
pros: Best user experience for static content
options.code_example: async function cacheFirst(req) { return (await caches.match(req)) || fetch(req); }

options.name: Network First (Network Falling Back to Cache)
options.description: Try network first, use cache as fallback
cons: Requires network attempt even when cached
options.code_example: async function networkFirst(req) { try { const res = await fetch(req); cache.put(req, res.clone()); return res; } catch { return caches.match(req); } }

options.name: Stale While Revalidate
options.description: Return cache immediately, update in background
options.code_example: Return cached, fetch() in background to update cache

opinions.title: Workbox vs Custom Service Worker
opinions.description: Whether to use Workbox library or write custom service worker code
opinions.rationale: Workbox provides battle-tested patterns and reduces boilerplate, but adds dependency. Custom service workers give full control but require more code and testing

when_applicable: Deciding on service worker implementation approach

options.name: Workbox
options.description: Use Google's Workbox library for service worker implementation

options.name: Custom Service Worker
options.description: Write service worker code from scratch

opinions.title: Installation Prompt Timing
opinions.description: When to show PWA installation prompt to users
opinions.rationale: Installation prompts should enhance user experience, not interrupt it. Timing affects acceptance rate and user perception

options.name: After Engagement (Recommended)
options.description: Show prompt after user has engaged with app (60+ seconds, multiple page views)

options.name: Contextual Prompt
options.description: Show prompt when user performs specific valuable action
cons: May not trigger for all users

options.name: Persistent Install Button
options.description: Provide subtle, always-visible install button (no automatic prompt)

opinions.title: Offline Experience Depth
opinions.description: How comprehensive the offline functionality should be
opinions.rationale: Offline capabilities range from simple fallback pages to full offline functionality. The right level depends on use cases and development resources

options.name: Full Offline Functionality
options.description: App fully functional offline with local data storage and sync

options.name: Read-Only Offline
options.description: Users can view cached content offline, but not create/edit

options.name: Offline Fallback Page Only
options.description: Show custom offline page when no connectivity
